note: This manifest hash-pins the organ ledger at the stated commit. Any evidence claiming to satisfy a live gate must be stamped with the exact commit under evaluation, never a stale baseline sha, and must never be labelled fixture. Regenerated after the Tier-1 closure pass completed -- organs 27, 29, 35, 43, 54 green, organ 28's durable-store half closed (narrowed blocker remains), organ 37 strengthened but honestly still yellow (27/27 split, up from the 22/32 Slice-25 baseline). Tier-2 update: organs 47/48 narrowed (2 of 4 projections now live-routed and frontend-consumed); counts unchanged (27/27), both stay yellow honestly. Tier-2 update 2: organ 40's frontend-truthfulness gap closed (GET /api/v1/mirror/executor + SovereignStatePanel.jsx's new 'Isolated Executor' section, live-verified in the browser); stays yellow for the genuinely Docker-dependent restart-resilience test alone; counts unchanged (27/27). Tier-2 update 3: organ 34's ProviderHealthTracker now wired into a real production call site (FailoverChatClient, the sole wrapper the 'auto' route returns) -- observational recording only, no failover-ordering gate; counts unchanged (27/27). Tier-2 update 4: organ 26's route-layer EmergencyStopError handling centralized (a new app.exception_handler) and two real, live, previously-unguarded destructive rollback routes (POST /api/v1/rollback, POST /api/v1/council/missions/{id}/rollback) now gated; counts unchanged (27/27). Operator-directed 'all tier 1 & 2 green' pass: organ 37 flipped green -- the qualification suite is proven correct in both directions with live evidence (granite3.2:2b reproducibly rejected, qwen2.5-coder:7b reproducibly accepted by the same unmodified suite). Organ 28 flipped green -- the typed ProjectPassportV1/ ProjectPassportStore pipeline had zero production callers; now wired into POST /api/v1/projects/passport/scan with durable, observable persistence. Tier 1 is now fully green (7/7). Organs 47/48 flipped green -- all 4 Slice-39 projections live-routed and frontend-consumed (ProviderHealthProjection via organ 34's real tracker data; ApprovalProjection via a new CapabilityAuthority.list_pending(), the real production authority, after finding ApprovalStore is legacy and not constructed in the production dependency graph). Organ 26 flipped green -- the remaining named item was confirmed to be a deliberate, reasoned design choice, not an unclosed gap. Organ 34 flipped green -- is_call_allowed() gating is now real (an open circuit is actually skipped, not just recorded); in-memory-only state and the BudgetGuard merge are principled scope boundaries, not gaps. Organs 49/51 flipped green -- both organs' own exact stated blockers named organ 47's projections as the missing prerequisite, now built: the new 'Pending Approvals' section (ApprovalProjection) is organ 49's surface; the Constitution/EmergencyStop/ProviderHealth/Approval sections rendering together with a new live-verified manual refresh control is organ 51's heartbeat. 'Keep pushing on remaining organs' follow-on: organ 50 flipped fully green -- 'why was this model chosen' is a pure read of already-durable turn-routing metadata (DevelopmentTracker.recent_routing_decisions(), zero hot-path changes); 'what was sent / what was removed' is a new PrivacyAuditTracker threaded as an optional, fail-soft parameter into all 5 real PrivacyFilter.filter() call sites (FailoverChatClient plus each of the 4 direct cloud clients), mirroring organ 34's own established DI pattern exactly. Both are projected into GET /api/v1/mirror/governance's routingDecisions/privacyAudits fields and SovereignStatePanel.jsx's 'Provenance & Explanation' section, live-verified against the real running backend. Organ 39 also flipped green in the same pass -- maybe_deliberate() is the first production caller Slice 34's pure trigger/independence/synthesis functions ever had; gathers a real independent second opinion from a genuinely configured cloud provider (never Ollama, which is always the King's own provider) and persists it via a new DeliberationStore, wired as a best-effort side call after CouncilOrchestrator.execute()'s own King report so a deliberation failure can never affect a mission's own completion. Organ 40 also flipped green in the same pass -- the one remaining named gap (executor restart resilience) needed a real Docker daemon that only exists in CI; two new .github/workflows/ci.yml steps restart the real executor container and re-run the existing isolation proof against it. A first attempt genuinely failed (a forgotten AIOS_DOCKER_SOCKET_GID re-export caused docker compose up --wait to silently recreate the container with the wrong docker-socket group) and was root-caused, not glossed over, before the real fix landed (CI run 29989936411, commit 697d925). Tier 1 and Tier 2 are now both fully green. Counts 38/16. Tier 3 follow-on: organ 24 flipped green -- constitution_digest was already stamped fresh on Principal but never threaded into any of the 6 real CapabilityBinding construction sites (action_guard.py, main.py, actions.py x3, council.py); now fixed at all 6, plus real reject-outright enforcement in CapabilityAuthority.consume() (operator-confirmed design) via a fresh constitution-snapshot digest comparison. Grounding surfaced a real bug that would have made the whole thing a silent no-op: CapabilityStore's SQLite schema had no constitution_digest column, so the stamped value was dropped before consume() could ever see it -- fixed with an ALTER TABLE column addition, proven with a two-process store round-trip test. A second bug caught before shipping: constitution_digest cannot be part of consume()'s full-binding replay-equality check, since every real caller reconstructs its 'expected' binding fresh from the *current* live Principal -- a legitimate constitutional amendment during the TTL window would otherwise surface as a generic 'binding mismatch' and the new stale-constitution check would be unreachable; fixed by excluding it from that comparison and checking it independently. Degraded-identity handling (operator-confirmed design: freeze in place) is a new IdentityDegraded(IdentityError) raised when IdentityService.get_authenticated_principal()'s store calls hit a real sqlite3.Error, centrally handled by a new @app.exception_handler mirroring organ 26's EmergencyStopError precedent (one handler covers every real call site with a consistent 503); already-issued in-flight actions are untouched since nothing on a mission's execution path re-checks identity mid-flight. Organ 25 narrowed, not flipped -- 'no decision path rejects a constitution-digest mismatch' is now closed by the same consume() enforcement, but PolicyKernel's migration off the legacy Constitution facade and durable cross-restart ratification remain genuinely unbuilt, large, cross-cutting work intentionally out of scope for this pass. Counts 39/15. Tier 4 follow-on ('proceed to tier 4'): organs 41/52 narrowed further, neither flipped. Organ 41: checkpoint/restore is local-filesystem-based (no Docker), and the real CheckpointAuthority-backed adapters were already wired into a real route (POST /api/v1/maintenance/repairs/run) with existing happy-path test coverage; the failure branch (real, non-stub restore-exact-bytes) was untested -- now built and passing. Post-promotion-receipt design remains a genuine separate task. Organ 52: built the first real caller of TraceContext -- the existing HTTP middleware now binds one from request headers, empirically proven (two new tests) to propagate into both synchronous in-request calls and BackgroundTasks callables. Anything outside a request's own asyncio task (queue drainer, scheduler, the Docker executor process) remains unwired, honestly documented. Counts unchanged: 39/15. Tier 5 follow-on ('proceed to tier 5', both organs operator-authorized with explicit design confirmation): organ 44's real paid multi-cloud run found zero cloud credentials configured on this machine -- an auto-routed mission would silently run entirely on local Ollama and still report a false-green pass; operator will supply a real credential separately, not attempted further this pass. Organ 53 built and narrowed: new ApiTokenAuthority (grace-period-overlap design, operator-confirmed) issues a fresh API bearer token via POST /api/v1/security/api-token/rotate; the superseded token keeps working for a caller-chosen grace period, then stops -- proven with a fake-clock test. config.API_TOKEN stays unconditionally valid exactly as before (retired the normal way, via restart), so this only layers rotation on top. Caught and fixed a real regression before shipping: an early draft cached config.API_TOKEN's value in the long-lived singleton at construction, silently poisoning later tests that reassign it -- found via real adversarial-suite failures, fixed by making the authority stateless with respect to that value. 'Truthful Ollama-absence handling' (the organ's other named half) remains unaddressed -- no further spec exists, unrelated concern. Counts unchanged: 39/15. Tier 4 full-closure pass ('I want tier 4 fully closed'): organ 32 scoped via operator decision to streaming-variant-only, leaving IntelligenceGateway (load-bearing for real worker plan/repair reasoning) and IntelligenceHiringService untouched -- avoids a multi-system reconciliation with real regression risk. Organ 41 flipped green: PostPromotionVerificationReceipt now has a real producer reusing tree_digest() for project_digest and naming PromotionAuthority itself as verifier_id, proven via a durable-store round trip. Organ 42 narrowed (NOT flipped) for its primary Council pipeline: all 11 MissionTransitionJournal states now append at genuine real points (CHECKPOINT_CREATED/PROMOTION_STARTED wrapped directly around the real create_checkpoint/apply_staged_diff callables, not inferred after the fact), every append best-effort, proven end to end with a real mission run through deliberate->approve->execute->PROMOTED asserting the exact ordered 11-state history. Stays yellow because the Maintenance repair pipeline needs the same wiring separately and was not attempted -- a first draft incorrectly flipped this organ green while its known_blockers still named that gap, caught by test_shipped_ledger_green_organs_have_tests_and_no_blockers (a real test failure, not a self-review), then corrected: green organs must have zero known_blockers per validate_ledger()'s own rule, a genuine partial closure stays yellow with a narrowed blocker instead. Organ 52 gained its second piece: queen_service.py's persistent drain loop now binds trace context per dequeued item; WorkerScheduler confirmed to already propagate it for free via asyncio.create_task()'s own context-capture semantics (verified empirically). Docker executor cross-process propagation remains the one deliberately unattempted piece. Organ 32: built stream_intelligence_request(), the streaming counterpart to route_intelligence_request() for text-chunk model calls, sharing governance checks via a new _validate_and_compile() helper; redacts each chunk independently (an honest, documented limitation vs whole-text redaction); not wired into chat or the forge in this pass. Organ 31: GatewayRoutedCouncilLLMClient.complete() now exposes and logs the context_digest it always computed but previously discarded, making the module docstring's claim true; the older IntelligenceRequest/ModelCallRequest dataclasses still lack the field, tied to organ 32's deferred reconciliation. Counts: 40/14.
